taxonomy: Pithecellobium confusum is a species of plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legume or pea family. It is a part of the subfamily Faboideae. Further classification places it in the tribe Ingeae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its relationship to other species within the family. As a member of the Ingeae tribe, Pithecellobium confusum shares similarities with other species in this group.
uses: Pithecellobium confusum, also known as the "jatoba" tree, has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. Its bark and leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating various health issues.
